# Diagrams / Drawings

When asked for a diagram, drawing, architecture diagram, flowchart, system map, or "draw me ...":

**Standard: modern, dark-mode, exported to PDF (+ PNG for inline preview). NEVER use mermaid — Brad considers it ugly/not modern.**

## Tool choice
- **Default → D2** (`d2`): text → polished dark diagram, native PDF+PNG.
- **Icon-rich cloud/infra → mingrammer `diagrams`** (Python; interpreter `~/.diagram-tools/venv/bin/python`, needs Graphviz `dot`).
- **Fully bespoke visual → hand-authored SVG → `rsvg-convert`**.

## Output convention
Write into the project's `diagrams/` folder. Produce `<name>.pdf` (primary, dark) + `<name>.png` (inline preview) + keep the source (`.d2`/`.py`/`.svg`). In markdown: embed PNG, link PDF.

## Commands
```bash
# D2 (default) — dark themes: 200 Dark Mauve, 201 Dark Flagship Terrastruct
d2 --theme 200 --pad 40 diagrams/<name>.d2 diagrams/<name>.pdf
d2 --theme 200 --pad 40 diagrams/<name>.d2 diagrams/<name>.png

# mingrammer diagrams — set graph_attr bgcolor "#0b0f17" + light fontcolor for dark mode
~/.diagram-tools/venv/bin/python diagrams/<name>.py   # Diagram(..., outformat=["pdf","png"])

# bespoke SVG → PDF/PNG
rsvg-convert -f pdf -o diagrams/<name>.pdf diagrams/<name>.svg
rsvg-convert -f png -z 2 -o diagrams/<name>.png diagrams/<name>.svg
```

Claude Code: a richer `/diagram` skill exists at `~/.claude/skills/diagram/`. Always read the rendered PNG back to self-check (overlaps, legibility, dark mode) before reporting done.
